How to fight this boss? He kept sealing all my command cards and exhalt

which stage is that? usually seal bosses either only do it on a specific turn, or they have the chain buff on them and will seal whoever attacks them first in the turn(sometimes more than 1 stack so first 2 or 3 hits for ex). Alva can also be used as a support to remove seals depending on the situation
This is Hard mode chap 7-4
He keeps replenish the seal every turn, and once I use posse he will also seal all the exhalt 💀
Guess I can't use Tawil for this specific boss
you just attack with someone else first
like ryker or karen
he has some moves that add more chains for some turns
but Ryker multihit can pop them all(he won't be able to do shit for several turns though)
you just need to make sure you never attack with Tawil first(also monowill can dig for Alva's seal removal card)
Aight let me try. Thanks for the tips.
Combat Stance, the 0 cost is the name of the card
removes all seals, so you could like pop a bunch of them with Ryker multihit then play that on Tawil to remove them if you need Ryker to do something
but having Ryker do nothing should be fine for the boss fight, just need Hameln and Tawil

Haiyoo, wanna ask if its possible to get gmurphy e3 with 160 pull or should i save it for future char
dont have his wheel tho, is there anything to use instead of it?

you're in arc1 you should be using shop wheels like Emerge(volcano) and Frenzy(eye) and Gluttony(teeth) if you're having survivability issues, mostly for late arc1 on Caecus/Ramona especially. 

Signature wheel aren't necessarily the best for the characters either so doesn't really matter, Ryker's wheel isn't particularly good, just give him the highest crit wheel you have

It's possible to get E3 in 10pulls, but the average is about 41pulls per copy, so yes on average E3 would be 160pulls total, that said averages aren't necessarily going to hit, I'd say pull at least E0 as she's very good regardless, and wait until kit reveal for Mouchette later this week to decide if you go further or save for next char

What's "Faded Legacy" and "Astral Reign"? I heard those words somewhere but forgot and the wiki is silent about it
Faded Legacy is arc 1, Astral Reign is arc 2.

basically they're two different rulesets that stages follow

Faded Legacy is for anything related to Arc 1, same for Astral Reign and Arc 2
After you complete chapter 9, you will unlock another story tab for Astral Reign.

Every awakener has a talent called Soulforge, which will only be active on stages that use the arc 2 (Astral Reign) ruleset.
<@967406876029501462> You can click on it to see their rules and relics.

Probably for stages with multiple fights, but in this case I only have one single fight against a single enemy 

For Pollux, he's basically a DPS who really likes using his extra card "Sacred Heart" - playing it gives him a special stacking buff that makes his damage inflict Bleed

Same "Sacred Heart" can be used up to 3 times before being Exhausted, and after third use, the next card he uses gets triggered twice

Skill 1 - 2-cost AoE damage that draw 2 cards with lowest Arith cost

Skill 2 - 3-cost 2-hit ST damage, -1 cost for every "Sacred Heart" played, reset after being played

Exalt - AoE damage, puts 1 "Sacred Heart" into hand, next card played gains boosted stats

Are symptoms from early main story chapters meant to be negative or at least trade offs? Because the most consequential ones i've seen so far are -10 HP +some effect and even those are mostly negligible. I sometimes suspect i don't understand the real impact of an effect, but e.g. here <screenshot> i straight up don't know where to *look for* a downside since hand size is unconstrained
hand size is in fact constrained, you have a base max hand size of 10
But yeah mania is one of the best symptoms
although i do admit it was like 4am and i don't have a perfect memory
you most likely have a wheel on your team that increases the max to 12
such as thais wheel
i'm pretty sure i had a god daym giant hand when fighting the wax museum reception lady who spammed my hand with wax
then there are certain stages and relics that give you a bigger hand size
as for downside
the downside of adding symptoms to your deck is they dilute your hand
you dont draw your good cards
1 or 2 symptoms is manageable but if you draw like 5 cards and they are all symptoms
you arent doing anything that turn
and also they all usually have tradeoffs
that's so true
-10 hp sucks actually if your whole hand is cards like that
didn't think of that
i honestly didn't focus on the "hand" as a concept at all until now
ty
i'm pretty sure this is a mistranslation as well

the source text is
> 抽到后若本回合剩余的算力大于等于2点，下回合额外抽2张牌。
When drawn, if you end this turn with 2 or more Arithmetica remaining, additionally draw 2 cards next turn.
yeah reading the in-game variant more closely your translation makes more sense
since arithmetica is always gonna be >2 at turn start
(..right?)
you might be right
how foolish of me to assume that the card effect is what is written on the card
